summary: Update to latest MongoDB Java driver 3.5
Cache SCRAM-SHA-1 ClientKey
Improve error messages when decoding arrays or documents
description:
See https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44627621/use-mongo-scala-driver-macros-to-read-documents-with-additional-nested-fields

When encountering an unexpected {{List}} or {{Document}} when decoding data it throws a:

bq. java.util.NoSuchElementException: head of empty list

The {{typeArgs}} list should be inspected to ensure it is the expected datatype and a bson exception should be thrown.

summary: Support Salat style @Key annotation.
description: PR: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/pull/32

summary: Add implicit conversion for future option

[This commit|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/commit/e7182bfe595a9f617f7d50bc05423da92b58959a] forgot to handle extra arrays. When I try to load a case class from an underlying document containing an extra array field, I get this error:

summary: Add support for a "comment" parameter to the aggregate command
Update to the MongoDB Java driver 3.6
Codec Registry case class ordering
description:
I've been trying to track down a strange issue which happened during upgrading from version 2.0.0 to 2.1.0. I haven't been able to reproduce a small example of what's happening yet, it only occurs in our production code which I unfortunately can't share. I'll continue to try to work out an example on Monday, until then I'll try to describe what's happening.

We have 5 case classes which represents our model. The case classes are nested (and some are recursive, e.g. A has a B inside it and B has an optional A inside it).

(Everything works perfectly in 2.0.0 but I wanted to upgrade to 2.1.0 in order to handle non-existing fields as None.)

We create our codec registry like this:
{code:scala}
val codecRegistry =
    fromRegistries(
      fromProviders(
        classOf[A],
        classOf[B],
        classOf[C],
        classOf[D],
        classOf[E]
      ),
      DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY
    )
{code}

Turns out that in 2.1.0, the ordering of the classOf apparently matter (and is treated differently). If I use the same ordering as we had in 2.0.0, then we'll crash on this line:

https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/blob/27089cc446b15061efdf16850804fe1a375d37c2/bson/src/main/scala/org/mongodb/scala/bson/codecs/macrocodecs/MacroCodec.scala#L204

Due to fieldClazzTypeArgs being empty. This seem to happen because some of the case classes aren't marked as actually being case classes in the "classToCaseClassMap".

If I change the ordering which I pass in the classOf[...], I can make them all be marked as being case classes. This feels sketchy though and I haven't found a reason for why this ordering matters or why it would be different between 2.0.0 and 2.1.0.

description: As part of the Java 9 modularity project in Java driver 3.7, the MongoDriverInformation class was moved to the {{com.mongodb}} package.  The Scala driver must change its import to reflect that.

summary: Deprecate snapshot option
Spec tests should use the same format for bulk writes
description:
There are three changes, the first two are breaking. 

The two breaking changes are in three command-monitoring tests: "bulkWrite.yml", "unacknowledgedBulkWrite.yml" and "insertMany.yml":

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/bebf9b688f64afdec46a860f102bad0841b04c02

The format of the "requests" array and "ordered" option changes from this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - insertOne: 
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        ordered: false
{noformat}
To this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - name: "insertOne"
            arguments:
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        options:
          ordered: false
{noformat}
This brings Command Monitoring tests in line with the Retryable Writes and forthcoming Transactions tests.

The non-breaking change is in the Retryable Writes tests:

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/c0ce96daac5661f2603bfa52c9b3f683c176a292

Before, the tests said in prose that all MongoClients should be created with retryWrites=true. Now, the tests include the following YAML:
{noformat}
        clientOptions:
            retryWrites: true
{noformat}
The clientOptions are the same for all Retryable Writes tests. Interpreting this field now will prepare you for the Transactions tests, which make real use of the field.

summary: Update ChangeStream spec to detail usage of startAtOperationTime
description:
If drivers have already implemented DRIVERS-464, they need to update their implementation of how they initially store {{startAtOperationTime}}. Instead of setting a default {{startAtOperationTime}} when the ChangeStream is created, drivers must omit a default {{startAtOperationTime}}. When resuming, drivers must use the {{operationTime}} of the initial aggregate command as the default {{startAtOperationTime}} value.

See https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/745debcf4624ec820eb9b138cd8f28fa7b1e5782 for the exact implementation details.

summary: Fix order issue with ExecutionContextObservable
description:
As it uses an ExecutionContext there can be a race condition between calls of the methods.

{{onSubscribe}} and {{onNext}} need to block to ensure that they have been processed before allowing further calls to the Observable. {{onError}} and {{onComplete}} do not need to block as they are only permitted to be called once and are terminal methods on the observer.

summary: Provide Transactions example for Docs
Document SCRAM-SHA-256
Macro support for tagged types in case classes
description:
Hi!

We've been using tagged types in our code base but unfortunately they are not supported in the current codec generator macro.
 Case classes having fields with tagged types are rejected by the Scala compiler simply because we cannot take `classOf` of a tagged type:
{code:scala}scala> classOf[String with Tag]
<console>:13: error: class type required but String with Tag found{code}
If it's acceptable as an enhancement, I'll be happy to submit a patch for this.
 —
 Just for completeness, tagged types are something like this:
{code:scala}trait Tag
type EmailAddress = String with Tag
val e: EmailAddress = "arbitrary string" // compilation error{code}
They introduce additional compile-time type information without changing the runtime representation of values.
 For more detailed introduction of tagged types please see e.g. [here|http://www.vlachjosef.com/tagged-types-introduction/].

summary: ToSingleObservable throws IllegalStateException for createIndexes(): SingleResultCallback[util.List[String]]
Make DB based future implicit operations run on the local thread
description:
Currently, the {{ObservableImplicits.headOption}} method doesn't set a context for running, so in the {{head}} method we use Scala's global execution context. If we use the current thread then it will use the same context/thread as the observable.

See: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/pull/45]

summary: Can't deserialize case class extending AnyVal
toFuture() and toFutureOption() randomly returning null/None
MongoClientSettings 404 / Builder documentation
description:
API Documentation for MongoClientSettings and the Builder can be found here:

[http://mongodb.github.io/mongo-scala-driver/2.4/scaladoc/org/mongodb/scala/MongoClientSettings$.html]

If you click on the linked MongoClientSettings classname, you are taken here:

[http://mongodb.github.io/mongo-scala-driver/2.4/scaladoc/org/mongodb/scala/index.html#MongoClientSettings=com.mongodb.MongoClientSettings]

Then if you click on "com.mongodb.MongoClientSettings" you get a 404 from this URL:

[http://api.mongodb.com/java/current/com/mongodb/MongoClientSettings.html#com.mongodb.MongoClientSettings]

 

Further, starting at the first link:

[http://mongodb.github.io/mongo-scala-driver/2.4/scaladoc/org/mongodb/scala/MongoClientSettings$.html] 

 

If you click on the "Builder" hyperlinked class name, it only collapses/uncollapses the method. The way it's linked, it seems like it should go to documentation on the Builder class, but I'm not sure what's intended. (Is there API documentation on the MongoClientSettings.Builder class? That seems important.)

summary: BsonArray.apply has useless overloads
description:
BsonArray.apply has two overloads to the `def apply(elems: CanBeBsonValue*): BsonArray` signature. 
The first one takes no parameters and is useless since it's the same as passing no args to the vararg signature.
The second one takes an `Iterable[BsonValue]` and this one tripped me up bad, because I wanted to call this one, but after some long debugging session found out I wasn't calling it at all. Instead, since any `Seq` will automatically be converted to a `BsonArray`, calling something like `BsonArray(List("Hello", "World"))` will result in `[["Hello", "World"]]`. The only way to call this method is to call it with something that is an `Iterable` but not a `Seq`, which is at the very least hard to come by. I suggest deleting the two overloads and adding a `fromIterable` function.

summary: Support for Scala 2.13.0
description:
scalatest has a 2.13 jar already released:

[`"org.scalatest" %% "scalatest" % "3.0.6-SNAP6"`|https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.scalatest/scalatest_2.13.0-M5/3.0.6-SNAP6]


The [`"org.scalamock" %% "scalamock-scalatest-support"`|https://github.com/jeremyrsmith/scalamock-scalatest-support] still needs 2.13 support.
